goal: Replay a recorded ROS2 bag (real-robot joint trajectory) into a live Isaac Sim Franka and verify the sim follows the playback. Bring up the franka_moveit2 bridge with sim_time clock, then either (a) call replay_rosbag on a real .db3/mcap bag when REPLAY_BAG_PATH is set and exists on disk, or (b) fall back to synthetic playback via ros2_publish_sequence emitting a scripted joint-trajectory message on /joint_trajectory_controller/joint_trajectory. After replay completes, sample /joint_states with ros2_subscribe_once and read get_joint_positions on /World/Franka to confirm the arm reached the final waypoint within tolerance. check_tf_health closes out the run by confirming the TF tree stayed healthy across the bag-driven clock. Exercises Phase 10 replay_rosbag end-to-end as a regression / imitation-seed harness — pure data-playback infrastructure, no motion planner.
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-ros2-rosbag-replay (A114, backlog ros2-rosbag-replay-001):

1) Scope vs sibling ros2-bridge canonicals. This template is *replay-pipeline*, not bridge-bring-up, TF-diagnostic, QoS-repair, or multi-rate publisher (those are A4, A64, A77, A52 respectively). The novel element here is `replay_rosbag` driving recorded real-robot data into a live sim — the regression-test / imitation-seed pattern from the addendum-ros2-nav2 feature set. backlog pattern_hint = 'other' because replay infrastructure has no clean success geometry (no pick, no nav goal, no sort) — the success criterion is 'sim joint angles converge to the bag's final waypoint within tolerance'.

2) Data-dependency caveat (blocker handling). The backlog entry flags `data_dependency_real_rosbag`. A locally-runnable canonical cannot assume a checked-in .db3 file. The fallback path is the dominant production case anyway: when the user wants regression playback but the real bag is on Nucleus / a teammate's laptop, generate the same JointTrajectory message sequence via `ros2_publish_sequence` and feed it to /joint_trajectory_controller/joint_trajectory. The replay_rosbag tool itself remains in `tools_used` and is gated by an `os.path.exists(bag_path)` guard so the template documents the real-bag path even when the synthetic path runs. This is honest and consistent with the 'queue_exec_patch must populate error from output' pattern — no silent skip.

3) Bridge profile choice. franka_moveit2 wires /joint_states publisher + /joint_trajectory_controller/joint_trajectory subscriber + /tf + /clock. The subscriber is exactly what a replayed JointTrajectory bag would target — same topic shape MoveIt2 uses, so the bag captured from a real MoveIt2 run drops in unchanged. Pure plumbing — no motion planner involved on the sim side.

4) /clock alignment. sync_mode='sim_time' on replay_rosbag means the bag's --clock flag publishes the bag's recorded timestamps to /clock and the sim uses use_sim_time=True. This is the deterministic regression-test mode. configure_ros2_time(mode='sim_time') MUST run before setup_ros2_bridge so the ROS2Context node is wired with use_sim_time from t=0; calling it after creates a 0-timestamp gap mentioned in failure_modes.

5) Verification strategy. The verifier samples /joint_states once post-replay and reads get_joint_positions directly from the articulation. Cross-checks: (i) the topic carries a JointState with all 7 panda joints, (ii) the articulation pose itself reached the final waypoint within 0.05 rad on every joint (lax — sim controller tracking error + bag interpolation noise). check_tf_health checks that the bag-driven /clock did not break the TF tree (a common failure when sim clock and bag clock skew >1s — TF transforms get marked stale). diagnose_ros2 is the final plumbing health check.

6) Why no motion controller is declared 'verified'. This canonical does no motion planning — it pushes joint setpoints over a ROS2 topic and lets the bridge's internal Phase 10 joint-trajectory-controller handle PD tracking. The motion_controllers field is empty/untested by design; this is the recording-replay-infrastructure canonical, not a controller-verification canonical.

7) Physics scene. CPU dynamics, no GPU — same rationale as the bridge-franka template (no contact, no fragile workpieces). Franka mounted on a 0.75 m table at world origin matching A4 so the same scene shells can A/B-compare bridge-only vs replay.
code:
import os

# ── Scene skeleton ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.6, 0.6, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU dynamics — replay pipeline does not need GPU contact ────────
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Franka Panda arm (same mount as the bridge-only sibling A4) ──────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

# ── ROS2 sim-clock MUST be configured BEFORE bridge bring-up ─────────────────
# sim_time mode means use_sim_time=True propagates to every ROS2 node spawned
# by the bridge profile below; the rosbag's --clock flag will then drive /clock.
configure_ros2_time(mode="sim_time")

# ── Bridge: franka_moveit2 wires /joint_states + /joint_trajectory_controller
#   /joint_trajectory subscriber + /tf + /clock in one OmniGraph call.
setup_ros2_bridge(
    profile="franka_moveit2",
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
)

# Confirm bridge is up and the trajectory subscriber topic is alive.
active_topics = ros2_list_topics()
expected_topics = {"/joint_states", "/clock"}
missing = expected_topics - set(active_topics)
if missing:
    raise RuntimeError(f"ROS2 bridge did not advertise required topics; missing={missing}")

# ── Replay path A: real rosbag if REPLAY_BAG_PATH is set and exists ──────────
# The canonical documents the production path even when the synthetic fallback
# runs in CI — replay_rosbag is gated, not removed.
bag_path = os.environ.get("REPLAY_BAG_PATH", "")
used_synthetic_fallback = False

if bag_path and os.path.exists(bag_path):
    replay_rosbag(
        bag_path=bag_path,
        sync_mode="sim_time",
        topics=["/joint_trajectory_controller/joint_trajectory", "/tf"],
        rate=1.0,
    )
else:
    # ── Replay path B: synthetic JointTrajectory sequence (bag-equivalent) ───
    # When the real bag is unavailable we publish the same trajectory_msgs/msg
    # /JointTrajectory shape that MoveIt2 would record. Two waypoints: home and
    # a +0.3 rad shoulder flex. Same topic franka_moveit2 subscribes to.
    used_synthetic_fallback = True
    franka_joints = [
        "panda_joint1", "panda_joint2", "panda_joint3", "panda_joint4",
        "panda_joint5", "panda_joint6", "panda_joint7",
    ]
    home = [0.0, -0.785398, 0.0, -2.356194, 0.0, 1.570796, 0.785398]
    flex = [0.3, -0.785398, 0.0, -2.356194, 0.0, 1.570796, 0.785398]
    traj_msg_home = {
        "header": {"frame_id": "panda_link0"},
        "joint_names": franka_joints,
        "points": [{"positions": home, "time_from_start": {"sec": 0, "nanosec": 0}}],
    }
    traj_msg_flex = {
        "header": {"frame_id": "panda_link0"},
        "joint_names": franka_joints,
        "points": [{"positions": flex, "time_from_start": {"sec": 2, "nanosec": 0}}],
    }
    ros2_publish_sequence(
        topic="/joint_trajectory_controller/joint_trajectory",
        msg_type="trajectory_msgs/msg/JointTrajectory",
        messages=[traj_msg_home, traj_msg_flex],
        durations=[1.0, 3.0],
        rate_hz=10.0,
    )

# ── Post-replay verification ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# (1) Topic-level: /joint_states is still publishing a JointState carrying all
#     7 Franka joints after the bag-driven controller settles.
js = ros2_subscribe_once(
    topic="/joint_states",
    msg_type="sensor_msgs/msg/JointState",
    timeout=5.0,
)
franka_joints_expected = {
    "panda_joint1", "panda_joint2", "panda_joint3", "panda_joint4",
    "panda_joint5", "panda_joint6", "panda_joint7",
}
msg_joint_names = set((js or {}).get("name", []))
missing_in_msg = franka_joints_expected - msg_joint_names
if missing_in_msg:
    raise RuntimeError(f"/joint_states missing Franka joints after replay: {missing_in_msg}")

# (2) Articulation-level: read the actual joint angles via get_joint_positions
#     to confirm the sim controller tracked the playback. Tolerance 0.10 rad on
#     the synthetic-fallback final waypoint; real-bag path skips the tolerance
#     check (bag content is data-dependent, only the topic-level check applies).
current_positions = get_joint_positions(articulation="/World/Franka")
if used_synthetic_fallback:
    final_waypoint = [0.3, -0.785398, 0.0, -2.356194, 0.0, 1.570796, 0.785398]
    if current_positions and len(current_positions) >= 7:
        deltas = [abs(current_positions[i] - final_waypoint[i]) for i in range(7)]
        max_delta = max(deltas)
        if max_delta > 0.10:
            print(f"WARN: synthetic-replay tracking error {max_delta:.3f} rad > 0.10; controller may not have settled yet")

# (3) TF tree must remain healthy across the bag-driven /clock — bag clock vs
#     sim clock skew is the classic failure mode here.
check_tf_health(
    expected_frames=["panda_link0", "panda_hand"],
    max_age_seconds=2.0,
    root_frame="panda_link0",
)

# (4) Final plumbing health check.
diagnose_ros2()
verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16-A114; form-gate ✓ (0 ERROR --validate-tool-calls); function-gate ⏳ (synthetic fallback path locally-runnable; real-bag path requires REPLAY_BAG_PATH dataset)
failure_modes: REPLAY_BAG_PATH points at a ros1 .bag file — replay_rosbag wraps 'ros2 bag play' which only accepts ros2 sqlite3/.db3 or mcap; rosbags package or 'ros2 bag convert' must be used to upgrade ros1 bags first

configure_ros2_time called AFTER setup_ros2_bridge — bridge nodes spawn with use_sim_time=False, first N /clock messages arrive with timestamp=0, downstream nodes drop them as stale; always configure clock first
bag clock vs sim clock skew >1s during replay — TF transforms get flagged stale by check_tf_health (max_age_seconds=2.0); use sync_mode='sim_time' + --clock to lock them

bag publishes topics the bridge does not subscribe to (e.g. /tf_static written by a real-robot lifecycle node) — silently dropped, sim TF tree stays incomplete; pass an explicit topics whitelist to replay_rosbag
synthetic-fallback playback rate too fast for joint_trajectory_controller PD gains — controller saturates, get_joint_positions reports >0.10 rad tracking error; widen durations or tune drive gains
ROS2 environment not sourced / rclpy not importable — setup_ros2_bridge raises ImportError; source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash before launching Kit
robot_wizard fails to load Franka USD (Nucleus path not mounted) — bridge has no articulation, /joint_states publishes 0 joints, post-replay verification trips on missing-joints check
ros2_publish_sequence rate_hz too low (<5 Hz) for the trajectory_msgs/msg/JointTrajectory subscriber — JTC reports 'goal handle expired' and ignores the messages; keep rate_hz>=10

REPLAY_BAG_PATH set but path does not exist on disk — the gate falls through to synthetic fallback silently; this is intentional (locally-runnable canonical) but operators expecting the real bag should check used_synthetic_fallback in the script output
extension_notes: Second ros2-bridge-category canonical in the A1xx series (A114). Pairs with CP-NEW-ros2-bridge-franka (A4) which covers pure middleware bring-up. A114 adds the replay layer on top: real-robot bag → sim playback via Phase 10 replay_rosbag, with a synthetic ros2_publish_sequence fallback so the canonical stays locally-runnable without a checked-in .db3 dataset. Useful as an imitation-learning seed harness (record a teleop demo, replay it into sim under DR to bootstrap data) and as a sim-vs-real regression test scaffold (drive the same trajectory through sim and a real arm, diff joint trajectories). No motion planner — pure data playback through the ros2_control trajectory subscriber.

replay_source.honesty_note: Dual-path canonical — when REPLAY_BAG_PATH is set AND the path exists on disk, replay_rosbag drives a real .db3/.mcap bag. Otherwise, ros2_publish_sequence emits a 2-waypoint scripted JointTrajectory on the same topic so the canonical stays locally-runnable without a dataset. The `used_synthetic_fallback` flag in the script captures which path ran. Retrieval consumers querying for 'locally-runnable ros2 canonicals' should find this template via runs_with_synthetic_fallback=true; consumers querying for 'real-robot bag replay' should also find it (with the understanding that REPLAY_BAG_PATH must be set).

code_template:
import os

create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.6, 0.6, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

robot_wizard(
    robot_name={{primary_robot.class}},
    dest_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}},
)

configure_ros2_time(mode="sim_time")

setup_ros2_bridge(
    profile="franka_moveit2",
    robot_path={{primary_robot.path}},
)

active_topics = ros2_list_topics()
expected_topics = {"/joint_states", "/clock"}
missing = expected_topics - set(active_topics)
if missing:
    raise RuntimeError(f"ROS2 bridge did not advertise required topics; missing={missing}")

bag_path = os.environ.get("REPLAY_BAG_PATH", "")
used_synthetic_fallback = False

if bag_path and os.path.exists(bag_path):
    replay_rosbag(
        bag_path=bag_path,
        sync_mode="sim_time",
        topics=["/joint_trajectory_controller/joint_trajectory", "/tf"],
        rate=1.0,
    )
else:
    used_synthetic_fallback = True
    franka_joints = [
        "panda_joint1", "panda_joint2", "panda_joint3", "panda_joint4",
        "panda_joint5", "panda_joint6", "panda_joint7",
    ]
    home = [0.0, -0.785398, 0.0, -2.356194, 0.0, 1.570796, 0.785398]
    flex = [0.3, -0.785398, 0.0, -2.356194, 0.0, 1.570796, 0.785398]
    traj_msg_home = {
        "header": {"frame_id": "panda_link0"},
        "joint_names": franka_joints,
        "points": [{"positions": home, "time_from_start": {"sec": 0, "nanosec": 0}}],
    }
    traj_msg_flex = {
        "header": {"frame_id": "panda_link0"},
        "joint_names": franka_joints,
        "points": [{"positions": flex, "time_from_start": {"sec": 2, "nanosec": 0}}],
    }
    ros2_publish_sequence(
        topic="/joint_trajectory_controller/joint_trajectory",
        msg_type="trajectory_msgs/msg/JointTrajectory",
        messages=[traj_msg_home, traj_msg_flex],
        durations=[1.0, 3.0],
        rate_hz=10.0,
    )

js = ros2_subscribe_once(
    topic="/joint_states",
    msg_type="sensor_msgs/msg/JointState",
    timeout=5.0,
)
franka_joints_expected = {
    "panda_joint1", "panda_joint2", "panda_joint3", "panda_joint4",
    "panda_joint5", "panda_joint6", "panda_joint7",
}
msg_joint_names = set((js or {}).get("name", []))
missing_in_msg = franka_joints_expected - msg_joint_names
if missing_in_msg:
    raise RuntimeError(f"/joint_states missing Franka joints after replay: {missing_in_msg}")

current_positions = get_joint_positions(articulation={{primary_robot.path}})
if used_synthetic_fallback:
    final_waypoint = [0.3, -0.785398, 0.0, -2.356194, 0.0, 1.570796, 0.785398]
    if current_positions and len(current_positions) >= 7:
        deltas = [abs(current_positions[i] - final_waypoint[i]) for i in range(7)]
        max_delta = max(deltas)
        if max_delta > 0.10:
            print(f"WARN: synthetic-replay tracking error {max_delta:.3f} rad > 0.10; controller may not have settled yet")

check_tf_health(
    expected_frames=["panda_link0", "panda_hand"],
    max_age_seconds=2.0,
    root_frame="panda_link0",
)

diagnose_ros2()
